top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Marco Pantani's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
78ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Gran Fondo Marco Pantani's paved Italian provincial roads through hilly forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 90 miles.
At mile 60 of 90, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
At Gran Fondo Marco Pantani, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Marco Pantani. Train it until it feels boring.
7,054ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Marco Pantani, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
7,054ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Italian provincial roads through hilly. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,054ft of climbing on paved Italian provincial roads through hilly does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 90 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Marco Pantani’s 90 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Marco Pantani requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved italian provincial roads through hilly romagnol and marche countryside to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 90 miles of paved italian provincial roads through hilly romagnol and marche countryside in Cesenatico, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
